title: Neighbors of Iran, Azerbaijan and Armenia fear regional spillover of conflict
subhed: Two Iranian drones struck Azerbaijan's Nakhchivan region on Thursday, raising fears of an expansion of hostilities in the South Caucasus, as Baku maintains close ties with Israel.

title: Iranian school hit by air strike: Le Monde's investigation confirms many civilian victims, including children
subhed: Videos obtained and analyzed by Le Monde confirmed that the bombing of a school in Minab, Iran, on the first day of joint US-Israeli strikes on the country, caused many civilian victims, including children.

title: Kurdish factions could weaken Iran's regime from within
subhed: As US-Israeli strikes continue, attention is turning to Iran's Kurdish units, now united in an unprecedented coalition. But the Kurds' history, marked by fragile alliances and betrayals, fuels both hope and caution.
byline: By Ghazal Golshiri and Allan Kaval

title: American consumers are already feeling the impact of the war against Iran
subhed: To mitigate the consequences of military operations launched in the Middle East on US inflation and economic growth, the Trump administration is seeking to ensure unimpeded shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and to implement insurance mechanisms.
byline: By Nicolas Chapuis (New York (United States) correspondent)

title: In Dubai, thousands of tourists left stranded by war in Iran
subhed: While 'very limited' airspace has reopened, the United Arab Emirates remains, with 196 missiles and more than 1,000 drones – most shot down in flight – the territory most targeted by Iranian strikes in the Middle East.

How Israel and the US are waging their most intense air war in decades on Iran
subhed: The coordinated US-Israeli forces have devised 'unprecedented' operations to establish their military dominance over Iran, destroy its missile launchers and factories and gain control of their adversary's airspace.

title: In rural France, a grisly death and a lingering question: Which of the dogs killed her?
subhed: Elisa Pilarski, 29 and six months pregnant, was found partially dismembered with dog bites in November 2019 in a northern French forest. She had been walking her partner's illegally imported pit bull. Christophe Ellul, the dog owner, is now standing trial and has been fighting against the idea that his dog was the sole killer.
byline: By Pascale Robert-Diard (Soisson (northern France), special correspondent)

title: Iran's neighbors, under drone attacks, confront their vulnerability
subhed: Lacking experience in countering kamikaze drones, especially the infamous Shahed models, the Gulf countries have shown how unprepared they were for the threat posed by Iran, which is determined to exploit their vulnerability.
byline: By Chloé Hoorman and Marie Jégo
